summary: Single-story rectangular small building with brick walls, a layered dark-red (netherbrick) stepped gable-style roof, stone corner piers, a white trim belt around the top of the walls, ground-level windows on the long sides, and a raised white low border platform framing the footprint.

shape.overall_form: compact rectangular box with a symmetrical stepped roof centered along the long axis
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetric along the long central axis

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: roof step caps (uncertain exact slab type)

elements: ground-level row windows on long sides (4 per side visible)
white continuous trim band around top of wall
stepped dark roof with slab/step caps on each tier
raised white border platform surrounding building footprint
rebuild_hints: Footprint: rectangular approx 9x15 blocks (estimate from aerial; adjust if counting exactly).
Walls: 3–4 block tall brick wall with a single horizontal white trim (quartz) band one block above window tops.
Windows: continuous series of 1x1 glass panes set in a row with one block spacing between panes; located in middle of wall height.
Corner pillars: use stonebrick (or similar) full blocks at each corner spanning full wall height up to trim.
Base skirting: a single layer of wood planks around bottom exterior between brick and ground.
Roof: symmetrical stepped gable built from netherbrick stairs/blocks, with 3–4 stepped tiers; place light-colored slab caps at the ends of each tier to match visible highlights.
Surrounding border: one-block-high white (quartz) low wall platform inset one block from roofline edges creating a rim around building.
uncertainties: Exact footprint block counts (width and depth) are estimated from aerial views; recommend counting if exact reconstruction required.
Material IDs: white trim and border appear to be quartz but could be another white block (e.g., smooth quartz or diorite); confidence high but not certain.
The stone corner blocks appear like stonebrick or andesite; exact variant uncertain.
Roof uses netherbrick stairs/blocks but exact use of slabs vs stairs for step caps is inferred from profile and not fully certain.
Number of windows per side is estimated from visible side views (4 visible) — far/hidden sides not fully shown but likely match symmetry.
